text.title: Accessibility as Code Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the Accessibility as Code methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; the last rule binds the decision tree skip path.

rule.statement: Component unit tests assert role + accessible name via @testing-library queries (`getByRole('button', { name: ... })`). Tests using `getByTestId` to bypass a11y queries are rejected.
rule.rationale: query by what users see, which is the AT contract.

rule.statement: Integration tests run axe on a named set of routes; fail on critical or serious severity. Skipping axe is rejected.
rule.rationale: axe is the canonical automated layer; without it, regressions slip.

rule.statement: CI a11y job is required on PRs; critical or serious axe violations block merge. Warning-only mode is rejected.
rule.rationale: Without blocking, the gate is decoration.

rule.statement: Known-issues / suppression list is namespaced per component + has an expiry. Global suppression is rejected.
Storybook stories cover error / focus / disabled / loading variants so axe can scan them. Happy-path-only stories are rejected.
Failing CI message links to the WCAG criterion or axe rule docs. Bare 'a11y check failed' is rejected.
rule.rationale: actionable failure messages get fixed; opaque ones get suppressed.

rule.statement: When the decision tree routes to `skip-this-methodology`, the agent MUST emit a structured `skip` decision (with reason + suggested-sibling) rather than running Accessibility as Code under degraded conditions.
rule.rationale: decision-tree contract. The decision tree is the only place that knows the routing; skipping must be observable so the orchestrator can log the routed-elsewhere path.
